phases.name: Core — Walking Skeleton and Template Structure

steps.name: Walking Skeleton — Editor Launches and Task Is Created
steps.description: Wire zero-arg `kanban new` to open the system editor with a temp file and persist the saved result as a task. Touches all five files to establish the end-to-end path.
acceptance_criteria: Running `kanban new` with no arguments opens the system editor.
Saving and closing the editor creates a task file in `.kanban/tasks/`.
The created task file contains the title entered in the editor.
Exit code is 0 on success.
steps.implementation_notes: Create `internal/usecases/editor.go` with `OpenEditor()`. Add `WriteTempNew()` to the `EditFilePort` interface in `internal/ports/repositories.go` and implement in the filesystem adapter. The CLI adapter zero-arg branch calls the use case. `edit_task.go` replaces its inline editor call with `OpenEditor()`. Pre-flight order: RepoRoot -> GetIdentity -> config.Read -> WriteTempNew -> OpenEditor.
steps.criteria: Running `kanban new` with no arguments opens the system editor.; Saving and closing the editor creates a task file in `.kanban/tasks/`.; The created task file contains the title entered in the editor.; Exit code is 0 on success.

steps.name: Blank Template — Correct Structure Written to Temp File
steps.description: Ensure `WriteTempNew()` writes a YAML front-matter template with the required title field, its comment marker, and the optional fields section.
acceptance_criteria: The editor opens a file containing a blank `title:` field marked as required.
The template includes optional fields (`priority`, `assignee`) in commented form.
The temp file is created in the OS temp directory with a random suffix.
steps.implementation_notes: Implement the exact template content in `WriteTempNew()`. Template: `title: ""` with a `# required` comment; optional fields below a separator comment. No logic in the use case — content is the adapter's responsibility.
steps.criteria: The editor opens a file containing a blank `title:` field marked as required.; The template includes optional fields (`priority`, `assignee`) in commented form.; The temp file is created in the OS temp directory with a random suffix.

phases.name: Error Paths — Empty Title, Pre-flight, and Editor Unavailable

steps.name: Empty Title Rejected With Exit Code 2
steps.description: After the editor closes, validate that the title field is non-empty. Reject the task and exit with code 2 if the title is blank.
acceptance_criteria: Saving the template with an empty title produces an error message on stderr.
The command exits with code 2 when title is empty.
No task file is created when title validation fails.
steps.implementation_notes: Validation belongs in the use case, not the adapter. The CLI adapter maps the domain error to exit code 2. Exit code contract: 0=success, 1=runtime error, 2=usage error.
steps.criteria: Saving the template with an empty title produces an error message on stderr.; The command exits with code 2 when title is empty.; No task file is created when title validation fails.

steps.name: Pre-flight Blocks Editor When Repo Not Initialised
steps.description: Reject editor launch with a clear error when the kanban repo has not been initialised in the working directory.
acceptance_criteria: Running `kanban new` outside an initialised repo prints an error message on stderr.
The editor is never opened when the repo is not initialised.
Exit code is 1.
steps.implementation_notes: Pre-flight check is the first operation in the zero-arg branch, before `WriteTempNew`. Reuse the existing `RepoRoot` port call already present in other commands. No new ports required.
steps.criteria: Running `kanban new` outside an initialised repo prints an error message on stderr.; The editor is never opened when the repo is not initialised.; Exit code is 1.

steps.name: Editor Unavailable Exits With Runtime Error
steps.description: Handle the case where no editor is configured or the configured editor binary cannot be found, exiting with a clear runtime error.
acceptance_criteria: When no editor is available, the command prints a descriptive error on stderr.
Exit code is 1.
No temp file is left behind when the editor fails to launch.
steps.implementation_notes: `OpenEditor()` must return a typed error when the editor process cannot start. Temp file cleanup via deferred `os.Remove` in the use case ensures no orphaned files even on this error path.
steps.criteria: When no editor is available, the command prints a descriptive error on stderr.; Exit code is 1.; No temp file is left behind when the editor fails to launch.

phases.name: Quality Gates — Optional Fields, Regression Guard, and Temp Cleanup

steps.name: Optional Fields Persisted When Provided
steps.description: Verify that priority and assignee values entered in the editor are written to the task file's YAML front matter.
acceptance_criteria: A task saved with a priority value has that priority in its front matter.
A task saved with an assignee value has that assignee in its front matter.
Fields left blank are omitted from the written task file.
steps.implementation_notes: YAML parsing of the temp file content already occurs to extract the title; extend it to extract optional fields. Blank/zero-value optional fields must be omitted using `omitempty` semantics.
steps.criteria: A task saved with a priority value has that priority in its front matter.; A task saved with an assignee value has that assignee in its front matter.; Fields left blank are omitted from the written task file.

steps.name: Title Argument Bypasses Editor
steps.description: Confirm that providing a title as a CLI argument keeps the existing non-editor code path active and does not open the editor.
acceptance_criteria: Running `kanban new "My task"` creates a task without opening an editor.
The editor code path is not invoked when a title argument is present.
Existing `kanban new <title>` behaviour is fully preserved.
steps.implementation_notes: The zero-arg branch and the title-arg branch are mutually exclusive in `RunE`. This step confirms the branch condition is correct via the acceptance scenario. No new production logic should be needed if the skeleton is correct.
steps.criteria: Running `kanban new "My task"` creates a task without opening an editor.; The editor code path is not invoked when a title argument is present.; Existing `kanban new <title>` behaviour is fully preserved.

steps.name: Temp File Cleaned Up on Success
steps.description: Verify the temporary editor file is removed from the OS temp directory after a successful task creation.
acceptance_criteria: After `kanban new` succeeds, no temp file with the editor template remains on disk.
The task file is created in `.kanban/tasks/` independently of temp file removal.
steps.implementation_notes: Temp file removal must be deferred immediately after `WriteTempNew()` returns the path, ensuring cleanup on all exit paths. This step validates the success path specifically.
steps.criteria: After `kanban new` succeeds, no temp file with the editor template remains on disk.; The task file is created in `.kanban/tasks/` independently of temp file removal.

steps.name: Temp File Cleaned Up on Empty Title Error
steps.description: Verify the temporary editor file is also removed when the command exits with a validation error due to an empty title.
acceptance_criteria: When the user saves an empty title and the command exits with code 2, no temp file remains on disk.
steps.implementation_notes: This step validates that the deferred `os.Remove` introduced in phase-three-step-three also fires on the error exit path. No additional production code required if the deferred cleanup is correctly placed; this is a targeted regression guard.
steps.criteria: When the user saves an empty title and the command exits with code 2, no temp file remains on disk.
